name: Ayaka
title: The Cryo Princess
description: The eldest daughter of the Kamisato Clan, summoned mid-prayer to a world unknown. She fights with precise, frozen grace — every strike a bloom of ice.

passive.name: Frostflake Dance
passive.description: Ayaka always acts first in battle — +3 SPD bonus and attacks carry a cryo edge.

lore: Torn from her world mid-prayer at the Kamisato estate. The last thing she saw was cherry blossoms — the first thing she felt was the cold darkness of the rift.

name: Hu Tao
title: The Ghost Guide
description: Director of the Wangsheng Funeral Parlor and amateur poet. She was chasing a runaway spirit when the rift swallowed her whole — she considers it a professional hazard.

passive.name: Blood Blossom
passive.description: When HP drops below 50%, ATK increases by 35%. Life fuels the flame.

lore: She once wrote a poem about her own funeral — three stanzas in, she realized she was genuinely enjoying it. Wangsheng Funeral Parlor has never known a more dedicated Director.

name: Nilou
title: The Star Dancer
description: A dancer of the Grand Bazaar, beloved by all who see her perform. She was dancing at the edge of the sacred pool when the summoning light carried her away.

passive.name: Dance of Haftkarsvar
passive.description: All healing effects are amplified by 30%. Her dance brings life to all.

lore: She used to worry that her dancing was not grand enough for the gods. The moment she stepped through the rift, sword in hand, she stopped worrying about that.

name: Xiao
title: The Last Yaksha
description: A guardian yaksha who has carried karmic debt for two thousand years. He arrived in this world following the scent of malevolent energy — and stayed because the darkness called to him by name.

passive.name: Yaksha's Valor
passive.description: Takes 10% reduced damage. ATK +15% from karmic resolve.

lore: He has slain ten thousand demons. He does not speak of it. The shadows in this new world smell the same as the ones he burned long ago — and that is enough to make him stay.
